prompt: Explain what a "closure" is in programming, in exactly one sentence. The sentence can be long.
response: A closure in programming is a function that retains access to its lexical scope (the variables and parameters from the surrounding context in which it was defined) even after that outer function has finished executing, allowing it to "remember" and use those variables later when invoked, effectively encapsulating a snapshot of the environment at the time of its creation.
